heading: Armed Forces Retirement Home Trust Fund
label: 419 Armed Forces Retirement Home Trust Fund
notes: Amendments2011—Subsec. (a)(2). substituted “Chief Operating Officer” for “Retirement Home Board”.Pub. L. 112–812008—Subsec. (d). added subsec. (d).Pub. L. 110–1812001—, inserted “Armed Forces” before “Retirement Home Trust Fund” in section catchline.Pub. L. 107–107, § 1410(a)(2)Subsec. (c). , substituted “Chief Operating Officer” for “Director”.Pub. L. 107–107, § 1404(b)(2)Subsec. (d). , struck out heading and text of subsec. (d). Text read as follows:Pub. L. 107–107, § 1410(b)(2)“(1) During the period beginning on , and ending on , the Fund shall contain a separate account for each establishment of the Retirement Home. During that period, contributions shall be collected under subsection (a) of this section for the account of the Naval Home for the purpose of achieving a trust fund five times the estimated annual operating budget of the Naval Home.November 5, 1990September 30, 1994“(2) Beginning on , funds required for the operation of the United States Soldiers’ and Airmen’s Home shall be drawn from the appropriate account. Beginning on , funds required for the operation of the Naval Home shall be drawn from the account of the Naval Home.November 5, 1990October 1, 1991“(3) During the period beginning on , and ending on —November 5, 1990September 30, 1994“(A) amounts collected as monthly fees paid by residents of the Naval Home and amounts referred to in subsections (a)(4) and (a)(5) of this section derived from enlisted members, warrant officers, and limited duty officers of the Navy, Marine Corps, and Coast Guard shall be credited to the account relating to that establishment; and“(B) amounts collected as monthly fees paid by residents of the United States Soldiers’ and Airmen’s Home and amounts referred to in subsections (a)(4) and (a)(5) of this section derived from members and warrant officers of the Army and Air Force shall be credited to the account relating to that establishment.”

children.text: Such amounts as may be transferred to the Fund.
Moneys deposited in the Fund by the Chief Operating Officer realized from gifts or from the disposition of property and facilities.
Amounts deposited in the Fund as monthly fees paid by residents of the Retirement Home under .section 414 of this title
Amounts of fines and forfeitures deposited in the Fund under .section 2772 of title 10
Amounts deposited in the Fund as deductions from the pay of enlisted members, warrant officers, and limited duty officers under .section 1007(i) of title 37
Interest from investments made under subsection (c).

children.heading: Availability and use of Fund
children.label: b Availability and use of Fund
children.text: Amounts in the Fund shall be available solely for the operation of the Retirement Home.

children.heading: Investments
children.label: c Investments
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury may invest in obligations issued or guaranteed by the United States any monies in the Fund that the Chief Operating Officer determines are not currently needed to pay for the operation of the Retirement Home.

children.heading: Reporting requirements
children.label: d Reporting requirements
children.text: The Chief Financial Officer of the Armed Forces Retirement Home shall comply with the reporting requirements of subchapter II of chapter 35 of title 31.
